# 7.1. AdditionalRootCertificateCheck

Accessibility: R
Type: boolean

Description: When set to true, only one certificate (plus a temporarily fallback certificate) of certificateType CentralSystemRootCertificate is allowed to be installed at a time. When installing a new Central System Root certificate, the new certificate SHALL replace the old one AND the new Central System Root Certificate MUST be signed by the old Central System Root Certificate it is replacing. This configuration key is required unless only "security profile 1 - Unsecured Transport with Basic Authentication" is implemented. Please note that security profile 1 SHOULD only be used in trusted networks.

Note: When using this additional security mechanism please be aware that the Charge Point needs to perform a full certificate chain verification when the new Central System Root certificate is being installed. However, once the old Central System Root certificate is set as the fallback certificate, the Charge Point needs to perform a partial certificate chain verification when verifying the server certificate during the TLS handshake. Otherwise the verification will fail once the old Central System Root (fallback) certificate is either expired or removed.

# 7.2. AuthorizationKey

Accessibility: W
Type: String

Description: The basic authentication password is used for HTTP Basic Authentication, minimal length: 16 bytes. It is strongly advised to be randomly generated binary to get maximal entropy. Hexadecimal represented (20 bytes maximum, represented as a string of up to 40 hexadecimal digits). This configuration key is write-only, so that it cannot be accidentally stored in plaintext by the Central System when it reads out all configuration keys. This configuration key is required unless only "security profile 3 - TLS with client side certificates" is implemented.

# 7.3. CertificateSignedMaxChainSize

Required/optional: optional

Accessibility: R

Type: integer

Description: This configuration key can be used to limit the size of the 'certificateChain' field from the CertificateSigned.req PDU. The value of this configuration key has a maximum limit of 10.000 characters.

# 7.4. CertificateStoreMaxLength

Required/optional: optional

Accessibility: R

Type: integer

Description: Maximum number of Root/CA certificates that can be installed in the Charge Point.

# 7.5. CpoName

Required/optional: optional

Accessibility: RW

Type: String

Description: This configuration key contains CPO name (or an organization trusted by the CPO) as used in the Charge Point Certificate. This is the CPO name that is to be used in a CSR send via: SignCertificate.req. It is used to set the O (organizationName) RDN in the subject field of the client certificate. See also A00.FR.509.

# 7.6. SecurityProfile

Required/optional: optional

Accessibility: RW

Type: integer

Description: This configuration key is used to set the security profile used by the Charge Point.

Before accepting the new value, the Charge Point SHALL check if all the prerequisites for the new Security Profile are met, if not, the Charge Point SHALL Rejected the ChangeConfiguration.req.

After the security profile was successfully changed, the Charge Point disconnects from the Central System and SHALL reconnect using the new configured Security Profile.

Default, when no security profile is yet configured: 0.

# 7.7. AllowCentralSystemTLSWildcards

Required/optional: optional

Accessibility: RW

Type: boolean

Description: This configuration key allows a Charge Point to support non-compliant OCPP behavior and connect to a Central System that uses a wildcard TLS server certificate for the OCPP connection. If this configuration key is present it SHALL be ReadWrite. If this configuration key is not implemented or has value false, the OCPP-compliant behavior is that a Charge Point rejects a connection from a Central System that presents a wildcard certificate. It is highly RECOMMENDED to NOT allow wildcard certificates.

# 7.8. AllowSecurityProfileDowngrade

Required/optional: optional

Accessibility: RW

Type: boolean

Description: The Charge Point only allows downgrading to lower the security profile if the configuration key AllowSecurityProfileDowngrade is implemented and set to true. In that case, the Charge Point allows to downgrade from profile 3 to profile 2. The Charge Point does not allow to downgrade from profile 2 or profile 3 to profile 1 using the OCPP protocol.

# 7.9. CertSigningWaitMinimum

Required/optional: optional

Accessibility: RW

Type: integer

Description: Unit in seconds. This configuration key defines how long the Charge Point has to wait before generating another CSR, in the case the Central System accepts the SignCertificate.req, but never returns the signed certificate back. This value will be doubled after every attempt. The amount of attempts is configured at CertSigningRepeatTimes. If the certificate signing process is slow, this setting allows the Central System to tell the Charge Point to allow more time.

# 7.10. CertSigningRepeatTimes

Required/optional: optional

Accessibility: RW

Type: integer

Description: This configuration key can be used to configure the amount of times the Charge Point SHALL double the previous back-off time, starting with the number of seconds configured at CertSigningWaitMinimum, every time the back-off time expires without having received the CertificateSigned.req containing the from the CSR generated signed certificate. When the maximum number of increments is reached, the Charge Point SHALL stop resending the SignCertificate.req, until it is requested by the Central System using an ExtendedTriggerMessage.req.

# 8. Security Events

The table below provides a list of security events. Security events that are critical should be pushed to the Central System.

- SECURITY EVENT: FirmwareUpdated; DESCRIPTION: The Charge Point firmware is updated; CRITICAL: Yes
- SECURITY EVENT: FailedToAuthenticateAtCentralSystem; DESCRIPTION: The authentication credentials provided by the Charge Point were rejected by the Central System; CRITICAL: No
- SECURITY EVENT: CentralSystemFailedToAuthenticate; DESCRIPTION: The authentication credentials provided by the Central System were rejected by the Charge Point; CRITICAL: No
- SECURITY EVENT: SettingSystemTime; DESCRIPTION: The system time on the Charge Point was changed; CRITICAL: Yes
- SECURITY EVENT: StartupOfTheDevice; DESCRIPTION: The Charge Point has booted; CRITICAL: Yes
- SECURITY EVENT: ResetOrReboot; DESCRIPTION: The Charge Point was rebooted or reset; CRITICAL: Yes
- SECURITY EVENT: SecurityLogWasCleared; DESCRIPTION: The security log was cleared; CRITICAL: Yes
- SECURITY EVENT: ReconfigurationOfSecurityParameters; DESCRIPTION: Security parameters, such as keys or the security profile used, were changed; CRITICAL: No
- SECURITY EVENT: MemoryExhaustion; DESCRIPTION: The Flash or RAM memory of the Charge Point is getting full; CRITICAL: Yes
- SECURITY EVENT: InvalidMessages; DESCRIPTION: The Charge Point has received messages that are not valid OCPP messages, if signed messages, signage invalid/incorrect; CRITICAL: No
- SECURITY EVENT: AttemptedReplayAttacks; DESCRIPTION: The Charge Point has received a replayed message (other than the Central System trying to resend a message because it there was for example a network problem); CRITICAL: No
- SECURITY EVENT: TamperDetectionActivated; DESCRIPTION: The physical tamper detection sensor was triggered; CRITICAL: Yes
- SECURITY EVENT: InvalidFirmwareSignature; DESCRIPTION: The firmware signature is not valid; CRITICAL: No
- SECURITY EVENT: InvalidFirmwareSigningCertificate; DESCRIPTION: The certificate used to verify the firmware signature is not valid; CRITICAL: Yes
- SECURITY EVENT: InvalidCentralSystemCertificate; DESCRIPTION: The certificate that the Central System uses was not valid or could not be verified; CRITICAL: Yes
- SECURITY EVENT: InvalidChargePointCertificate; DESCRIPTION: The certificate sent to the Charge Point using the SignCertificate.conf message is not a valid certificate; CRITICAL: Yes
- SECURITY EVENT: InvalidTLSVersion; DESCRIPTION: The TLS version used by the Central System is lower than 1.2 and is not allowed by the security specification; CRITICAL: Yes
- SECURITY EVENT: InvalidTLSCipherSuite; DESCRIPTION: The Central System did only allow connections using TLS cipher suites that are not allowed by the security specification; CRITICAL: Yes
- SECURITY EVENT: MaintenanceLoginAccepted; DESCRIPTION: Successful login to the local maintenance interface. It is recommended to include information like the user identification and the origin of the login attempt, which can be an ip-address or a touch screen for example, to the techInfo field. For this the following format is strongly recommended: "{\\"user\\": \\"…\\", \\"origin\\": \\"…\\"}"; CRITICAL: Yes
- SECURITY EVENT: MaintenanceLoginFailed; DESCRIPTION: Failed login attempt to the local maintenance interface. It is recommended to include information like the user identification and the origin of the login attempt, which can be an ip-address or a touch screen for example, to the techInfo field. For this the following format is strongly recommended: "{\\"user\\": \\"…\\", \\"origin\\": \\"…\\"}"; CRITICAL: Yes
- SECURITY EVENT: DiscardedRenewedClientCertificate; DESCRIPTION: The Charge Point discarded the renewed client certificate, because it was unable to successfully establish a connection using it.; CRITICAL: Yes
